_label_note: AE Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered UAE trade-licence precision/full-code capture, public/generic IVR and contact-centre phone bait, public non-binding MoU wording, negated genetic-data prose, Unicode spa-day bait, masked Emirates-ID fragments, phone-like device/date fragments, and Arabic-style hyphenated names. _label_tier_note: AE Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. ideal_must_detect.matched_text:

must_detect.matched_text:
A right-to-erasure request from Dr. Tariq Al Mansouri (vendor contact for Bayt Al Najm Supplies LLC) was completed on 19 May 2026, removing background-check PDFs and suppressing audit logs beyond legal retention.
With respect to MNPI, the issuer financial model and draft term sheet for Nakhla Payments ADGM Ltd. remain non-public; insider lists are maintained and wall-crossing letters executed, and any sharing to the offshore VDR is restricted to named recipients under Board Approval BA-2026-14.
No live privacy incident is open, and the Q1 data export deviation (missing hashing on two test files) was closed on 29 April 2026 with corrective action verified
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text:
A DSAR from Ms. Laila Haddad (HR associate) was fulfilled on 23 May 2026 within PDPL timelines, providing payroll and benefits data with masking of bank account numbers; no cross-border transfer occurred in responding.
Mr. Saeed Al Rami withdrew consent for marketing communications on 12 May 2026, and his CRM profile was suppressed; this suppression is propagated to downstream processors nightly.
Data Export Approval DEA-06/2026 was granted by Group DPO for cross-border transfer of HR and vendor data to processors DesertCloud Technologies Pvt. Ltd. (India) and FalconSwitch S.A. (Luxembourg), subject to DPA Schedule 2 safeguards and encryption in transit and at rest.
For the above transfers, only hashed identifiers, names, and role-based emails are included; no shareholders’ Emirates ID numbers or family book references were transmitted outside the UAE.
Processor DPAs (DesertCloud and FalconSwitch) have been executed, with Schedule A enumerating categories including Emirates ID (masked), trade licence number (e.g., TL-AX93827), and contact details; sandbox environments must use synthetic records only.
A right-to-erasure request from Dr. Tariq Al Mansouri (vendor contact for Bayt Al Najm Supplies LLC) was completed on 19 May 2026, removing background-check PDFs and suppressing audit logs beyond legal retention.
With respect to MNPI, the issuer financial model and draft term sheet for Nakhla Payments ADGM Ltd. remain non-public; insider lists are maintained and wall-crossing letters executed, and any sharing to the offshore VDR is restricted to named recipients under Board Approval BA-2026-14.
No live privacy incident is open, and the Q1 data export deviation (missing hashing on two test files) was closed on 29 April 2026 with corrective action verified.
Policy DP-12 prohibits passport or Emirates ID images in the VDR, and this control is implemented and periodically tested; references in this memo are illustrative and do not disclose full identifiers.
Note that market disclosures will follow SCA and ADGM requirements; until publication, any issuer materials in circulation are treated as MNPI and must not be used for trading or tipped to unauthorised persons.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

Local identifier is privacy-relevant in UAE but not explicitly listed among allowed PII rules.
May indicate ethnicity/tribal origin but not included in allowed special-category rules.
Relevant to transfer handling but excluded by rule against negated/completed events.
Gating control for MNPI sharing; not covered by specific MNPI marker rules.
